reasoning: The chart fails on two critical levels. First, it does not answer the specific business question, which asks for the total revenue per year (2021-2024). Instead, it displays monthly trends, forcing the user to mentally sum up 48 data points to get the annual totals. Second, and more severely, the data mapping is incorrect. The 'data' array contains four series labeled by year, but they all share the same X-axis range (1-12, representing months Jan-Dec). This results in overlapping lines where, for example, January 2021, January 2022, etc., are plotted at the exact same x-coordinate. While a line chart can show seasonality, plotting multiple years as separate lines on a single month-based axis without distinguishing them clearly or aggregating them makes the visualization cluttered and misleading for comparing annual performance. Furthermore, the Y-axis limit cuts off the peak value of ~242k (June 2022) since the max y_lim is 200,000.
suggested_improvement: Create a bar chart with Years (2021, 2022, 2023, 2024) on the X-axis and Total Annual Revenue on the Y-axis. Aggregate the monthly data into yearly sums before visualizing.
